text.title: go-error-handling Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: fmt.Errorf("...: %v", err) — wrapping with %v instead of %w.
antipattern.symptom: errors.Is and errors.As at the boundary return false even when the chain should match; sentinel checks silently fail.
antipattern.root-cause: %v stringifies the inner error and drops the chain.
antipattern.fix: Replace `%v` with `%w` in every fmt.Errorf wrap. errorlint linter catches this automatically.

antipattern.description: Type assertions at HTTP boundary: `if ae, ok := err.(*AppError); ok { ... }`.
antipattern.symptom: After any wrap (`fmt.Errorf("...: %w", appErr)`), the type assertion fails; handler emits a generic 500.
antipattern.root-cause: Type assertions don't traverse the Unwrap chain.
antipattern.fix: `var ae *AppError; if errors.As(err, &ae) { ... }`. errorlint catches this too.

antipattern.description: Driver errors (pgx.ErrNoRows, sql.ErrNoRows, redis.Nil) propagating to handlers.
antipattern.symptom: Handler matches on `pgx.ErrNoRows`; couples HTTP layer to the database driver.
antipattern.root-cause: Repository forgets to map driver errors to AppError.
antipattern.fix: At every repository function exit, wrap driver errors: `if errors.Is(err, pgx.ErrNoRows) { return apperror.NewNotFound("user") }`.

antipattern.symptom: Mid-request mutations corrupt subsequent requests; tests pass in isolation, flake under load.
antipattern.root-cause: Shared mutable state in a global; one handler mutates Message, all others observe the change.
antipattern.fix: Replace each ErrXxx with a NewXxx() constructor returning a fresh instance.

antipattern.description: Status demotion on rewrap: wrapping an inner AppError(404) at a higher layer produces AppError(500).
antipattern.symptom: 404s reported as 500s in SLO dashboards; on-call paged for client errors.
antipattern.root-cause: Wrap function defaults to HTTPStatus=500 without checking the inner chain.
antipattern.fix: In Wrap(), `errors.As` the input; if it's already an *AppError, preserve its HTTPStatus and Code.
